members.name: Laura Chastain
members.title: NYEF Director
members.bio: Laura has been living and working in National Parks for almost 20 years. Her first encounter with Yellowstone National Park was during a SHRA conference hosted by Cool Works in the fall of 2009. In August of 2021, she moved to Yellowstone with her husband Mas and son Robert. Laura holds a degree in Public Administration from the University of Arizona and has worked for non-profits such as the American Red Cross and Lupus Foundation of Northern California, as well as city parks and recreation programs. In her career in National Parks, she has served as the Recruitment Manager for Yosemite and the General Manager/ED of the Chamber of Commerce for Grand Canyon. During her time in Grand Canyon, she helped establish the Grand Canyon Food Pantry non-profit, worked closely with the Grand Canyon PTA, and participated in the local Rotary. Laura and her family have always wanted to raise their son in a National Park environment, and the Gardiner School was a big deciding factor for them. She is eager to learn more about the local community and contribute where she can. NYEF’s mission and goals resonate with her values.

members.name: Not Filled
members.title: Treasurer
members.bio: We are currently seeking a treasurer!

members.name: Jess Warren
members.title: Board Member
members.bio:
My name is Jessica Warren, but most people call me Jess. I'm a mom to two wonderful kids, Maizie and Jimmy both attend Gardiner School. We live in Upper Mammoth, where I work as a Judicial Assistant at the Yellowstone Justice Center. My husband, Jim, is the Emergency Services Coordinator for the park. Jim and I met back in 2008 while working as rangers at Old Faithful.

Originally from Kalamazoo, Michigan, I've lived in several places over the years, including Arizona, California, Oregon, Washington, Montana, and Wyoming. I studied Political Science, Sociology, Criminal Justice, and Law Enforcement at Western Michigan University and Northern Arizona University.

We moved to Yellowstone in September 2022 from Grand Teton National Park for various reasons. Jim was offered his dream job, and I can now walk to work. We also wanted to live in a neighborhood full of kids, and the school commute is now less than 15 minutes. However, one of our concerns was that the new school district is not as well-funded as our previous one. To address this, we plan to get involved in the school and see how we can help.

In my free time, I love to travel, go boating, scuba dive, watch wildlife, and relax at tiki-bars. I also enjoy a lazy, rainy day every once in a while.

members.name: Miriam Watson
members.title: Board Member
members.bio: Miriam Watson is the Museum Curator for the Heritage & Research Center, Yellowstone National Park. She has worked for the National Park Service for over 20 years throughout the country. She received a B.A. from University of CA, Santa Barbara in Art History and Business Economics and her master’s degree from San Francisco State University in Museum Studies. She has two kids at Gardiner Elementary School and enjoys spending time with them hiking, skiing (downhill and cross country), and camping.
